nodes.title: Treat text-node targets as their parent element
nodes.why: Clicks can originate from text nodes, so we normalize to an element before matching.
nodes.tradeoff: One extra check, but delegation works on real nested text content.

nodes.title: Stop traversal at until boundary first
nodes.why: until acts as a hard boundary, so we do not match anything above it.
nodes.alternative: Check selector first, then apply until later.
nodes.tradeoff: Stricter rules, but behavior is predictable in deep trees.

nodes.title: Ignore branch exits early before matching

anchor.snippet: if (ignore && isIgnored(start, ignore, root, until)) return;

nodes.why: If the event comes from an ignored region, we skip all matching work.
nodes.alternative: Compute matched first, then filter ignored cases after.
nodes.tradeoff: Slightly more branching, but fewer unnecessary checks.

nodes.title: Ignore walk also respects until boundary
nodes.why: Ignore logic should not cross the same boundary rules used by matching.
nodes.alternative: Let ignore walk all the way to root every time.
nodes.tradeoff: More consistent semantics, with one extra condition in the loop.

nodes.title: Optionally stop propagation before calling handler
nodes.why: This gives callers control when delegated handling should block outer listeners.
nodes.tradeoff: More power for callers, but they must use it carefully.

nodes.title: Run once cleanup only after a successful match
nodes.why: once should remove the listener after a real handler call, not after unrelated events.
nodes.alternative: Remove listener on first event regardless of match.
nodes.tradeoff: Keeps behavior correct, with a tiny extra condition per handled event.
